$comment: c34 (D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-SIZE-INFERENCE, C 6.7.9p22): a `[]` array WITH an initializer infers its length from the initializer — a string literal yields decoded-bytes+1 (the NUL), a brace list yields the top-level element count. Completed ONCE in the semantic model (Pass 1.5) so layout/element-access/both-HIR-paths agree. Frontier: sqlite3.c:506 `const char sqlite3_version[] = SQLITE_VERSION;` (mirrored by `ver`). s="DSS" (inferred char[4]), a={2,7,11,22} (inferred int[4]), ver="9" (inferred const char[2]). i=pick(3) is a RUNTIME index (opaque across the un-inlined pick()); a[3]==22, s[0]=='D'==68, ver[0]=='9'==57. 22 + 68 - 57 + (57-48) = 42. RED-ON-DISABLE: without inference the `[]` decls fail to compile (S_NonConstantArrayLength); a wrong inferred size lands the element reads off-storage and the exit is not 42. The release/optimized arm keeps `i` a runtime value across pick() so the element-access path runs (no whole-expr const-fold).
